member.name: Sarah Green
member.synopsis: Sarah Green is the Liberal Democrat MP for Chesham and Amersham , and has been an MP continually since 17 June 2021.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Children: Social Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether she plans to update guidance on handling complaints in children's social care.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department wants to ensure that the process for children’s social care complaints is effective and straightforward for families to navigate. We also want to ensure that local processes work effectively and efficiently within local authorities, and tha...

writtenQuestions.heading: Emergencies: Mobile Phones
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what consideration he has given to the potential merits of using mobile phone alerts to warn of the dangers of cold water shock in hot weather.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Responsibilities for water safety sit with various Government departments and agencies. As part of its “Beat the Heat” campaign, the UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) includes a stay safe when swimming section, including links to the Royal Life Saving Soc...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Pensions Ombudsman: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what assessment his Department has made of trends in changes in waiting times for cases initially to be assigned at the Pensions Ombudsman over the past two years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Waiting times for cases to be initially assigned at The Pensions Ombudsman (TPO) have increased over the past two years, primarily due to sustained growth in demand, alongside a backlog of complex cases. TPO has implemented an Operating Model Review to im...

writtenQuestions.heading: Pensions Ombudsman: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what data his Department holds on average waiting times for (a) cases to be assigned and (b) decisions by the Pensions Ombudsman.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department does not centrally publish formal averages for case allocation or final decisions by the Pensions Ombudsman (TPO). However, TPO’s latest performance updates indicate that waiting times remain significant due to sustained demand. Recent data...

writtenQuestions.heading: Pensions Ombudsman: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of the Operating Model Review in reducing waiting times at The Pensions Ombudsman over the past year.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Pensions Ombudsman’s (TPO) Operating Model Review (OMR) has delivered record case closures in both 2024/25 and 2025/26. Key changes introduced through the OMR include ensuring that individuals complete the formal complaints process with their pension ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Suicide
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment his department has made of the American '988' Suicide and Crisis Lifeline model, and whether there are plans to develop a similarly integrated crisis line here.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department of Health and Social Care has indicated that it will not be possible to answer this question within the usual time period. An answer is being prepared and will be provided as soon as it is available.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Enterprise Investment Scheme
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of the withdrawal of previously granted Enterprise Investment Scheme relief on investor confidence.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government is focused on making the UK the best place to start and scale a business. That’s why at Budget 2025 we announced the doubling of investment limits and gross asset thresholds for the Enterprise Investment Scheme (EIS) and Venture Capital Tr...

writtenQuestions.heading: Special Educational Needs: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether her Department plans to introduce retrospective funding for schools that met special educational needs support costs during periods when Education, Health and Care Plan assessments were delayed by local
writtenQuestions.answerText: Mainstream schools are currently expected to meet the costs of supporting their pupils with special educational needs (SEN), up to £6,000 per pupil per annum. When the costs of additional support required for a pupil with SEN exceed that £6,000 threshold,...

writtenQuestions.heading: Private Education: VAT
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what proportion of the additional revenue raised through the application of VAT to independent school fees has been allocated to addressing funding gaps in maintained schools.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Ending the tax breaks previously enjoyed by private schools raises essential revenue that will be invested in our public services, such as the £1.7 billion increase to core school funding in 2026/27, meaning an increased total of £67 billion, compared to ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Tax Avoidance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what estimate she has made of the number of people subject to the loan charge who will have their cases settled following the independent review of the loan charge.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government accepted all but one of the independent review’s recommendations and in some cases we are going further. We are legislating a generous new settlement opportunity that will help those who have not yet settled to do so. Most individuals could...

writtenQuestions.heading: Schools: Bullying
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether the Department plans to make anti-bullying training mandatory for school staff.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Tackling and preventing bullying in schools is essential to ensure that schools can provide calm and inclusive learning environments. All schools are legally required to have a behaviour policy with measures to prevent all forms of bullying. They have the...

writtenQuestions.heading: Schools: Bullying
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what assessment she has made of the consistency of the implementation of anti-bullying guidance in schools.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Tackling and preventing bullying in schools is essential to ensure that schools can provide calm and inclusive learning environments. All schools are legally required to have a behaviour policy with measures to prevent all forms of bullying. They have the...

writtenQuestions.heading: Children: Social Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether she plans to update guidance on handling complaints in children's social care.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Departmental officials will be meeting with the Local Government and Social Care Ombudsman to better understand issues with the complaints process, but at present we are not planning to update the complaints guidance. We will, however, publish updated sta...

writtenQuestions.heading: Prisoners: Hunger Strikes
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what clinical monitoring arrangements are in place for prisoners who have been on hunger strike for more than 14 days.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Healthcare services in the prison estate are commissioned by NHS England. All prison healthcare providers are commissioned and contracted to use National Institute for Health and Care Excellence guidelines and have pathways of care in place for people ref...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Housing: Older People
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether the proposals in the Strengthening Leaseholder Protections Over Charges and Services consultation are intended to apply to event fees in retirement villages.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the answer given to Question UIN 95245 on 5 December 2025.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Housing: Older People
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether the Government plans to require retirement housing operators to provide leaseholders with more detailed annual reporting on how management fees, including any surplus or
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the answer given to Question UIN 95245 on 5 December 2025.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Housing: Older People
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what progress has been made towards publishing a formal Government response to the Older People’s Housing Taskforce report.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is committed to enhancing provision and choice for older people in the housing market and we will continue to consider this issue as we develop our long-term housing strategy. As set out in the Written Ministerial Statement I made on 26 Nov...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Prisoners on Remand: Health
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what assessment has been made of the potential impact of court backlogs on health outcomes for prisoners held on remand.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government inherited a justice system in crisis, with a record and rising open caseload of nearly 80,000 criminal cases waiting to be heard in the Crown Court and too many victims waiting years for justice. That is why the Government commissioned Sir ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Special Educational Needs: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to ensure that NHS services make reasonable adjustments to accommodate additional need when treating children with (a) SEND and (b) level 3 autism.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is dedicated to ensuring that National Health Services are accessible for everyone, including for children and young people with Special Educational Needs and Disabilities (SEND) and those who are autistic. In accordance with the Equality A...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Parkinson's Disease: Health Professions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what estimate he has made of the number of clinical specialists in Parkinson's disease that will need to be recruited in each of the next five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: No specific assessment has been made.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
